title: Aqueous chlorine dioxide mediated tempering of wheat grains: Evaluation of tempering potential, kinetics, and mechanism of Salmonella inactivation.
author_string: Balyatanda, Suhan B, Chakraborty, Snehasis, Subramanyam, Bhadriraju, Siliveru, Kaliramesh
abstract: The study evaluated the potential of aqueous chlorine dioxide (ClO<sub>2</sub>) for tempering wheat. Wheat kernels were tempered to 17 % moisture using aqueous ClO<sub>2</sub> (20.6-102.8 mg kg<sup>-1</sup>) for 0-24 h in a 2-factor mixed level full factorial design (5 × 8 = 40 runs). The highest reduction of 4.1 log<sub>10</sub> CFU/g was obtained at 102.8 mg kg<sup>-1</sup> at 24 h; while all exposures ≥12 h of 82.2 mg kg<sup>-1</sup> achieved >3 log reduction. The biphasic inactivation model was the best fit to describe ClO<sub>2</sub> induced Salmonella inactivation. The fast phase (k<sub>1</sub>) and slow phase (k<sub>2</sub>) rate constants ranged from 0.88 to 2.20 h<sup>-1</sup> and 0.056 to 0.139 h<sup>-1</sup>, respectively. Consequently, the time required to attain 3 log reduction at concentrations of 20.6, 41.2, 61.7, 82.2 and 102.8 mg kg<sup>-1</sup> were 79.2, 49.4, 24.8, 12.7, and 5.2 h, respectively. The inhibition zones widened from 10.0 (20.6 mg kg<sup>-1</sup>) to 20.6  mm (102.8 mg kg<sup>-1</sup>). Cell death through membrane damage, leakage, and DNA integrity confirmed that oxidative damage was the dominant lethal mechanism for Salmonella inactivation. Furthermore, the yield of straight grade flour from ClO<sub>2</sub> tempered (82.2 and 102.8 mg kg<sup>-1</sup>) wheat was 1.3-3.4 % higher compared to untreated wheat. Particle size distribution of all flours remained virtually identical, with ClO<sub>2</sub> and ClO<sub>2</sub><sup>-1</sup>residues remaining within LOD. Tempering with ClO<sub>2</sub> enhanced the lightness (L*) of the flour, increasing ΔL* by +2.4 to 2.9. Overall, ClO<sub>2</sub> tempering with 82.2 mg kg<sup>-1</sup> of wheat offered >3 log reduction within 12 h of tempering time while maintaining flour properties similar to control.
